stdout:
## Market Research — Kabukicho Survival Map (task-0007-locker-deep)

**Business:** kabukicho_survival_map | **Role:** market_research | **Task:** task-0007-locker-deep

Read-only research per the output contract; no repository files were modified.

---

### Facts (grounded in search results, cited)

**Existing dataset gap**
- Current dataset (`task-0002/latest.json`) has exactly 1 coin-locker entry: a generic "JR Shinjuku Station B1F Coin Lockers (East/Central East Gate)" — not broken out by exit/bank, and none inside Kabukicho itself.

**JR Shinjuku Station — locker banks by exit (station-wide, ~3,600 lockers total, large ones frequently gone by 10am)**
- East Exit Underground Passage: 8 lockers across 4 sub-locations near the ticket gates. [shinjukustation.com](https://www.shinjukustation.com/shinjuku-station-lockers-luggage-storage-and-delivery/)
- Lumine Est (1F, at the East Exit taxi-stand loop / along the department-store wall, plus inside near the gates) — largest concentration outside the East gates. [shinjukustation.com](https://www.shinjukustation.com/shinjuku-station-lockers-luggage-storage-and-delivery/)
- West Exit Underground Plaza: 131 lockers, 17 large — the single biggest locker bank at Shinjuku Station. [shinjukustation.com](https://www.shinjukustation.com/shinjuku-station-lockers-luggage-storage-and-delivery/)
- South Exit concourse: multiple banks parallel to/inside the ticket gates, plus additional banks in the basement via stairs/escalators. [shinjukustation.com](https://www.shinjukustation.com/shinjuku-station-lockers-luggage-storage-and-delivery/)

**Seibu-Shinjuku Station (closest station to Kabukicho's main gate)**
- North Exit: lockers near the ticket machines; cashless-only (Suica/PASMO or the "SPACER" app — no cash), ¥100/¥200/¥300/¥400 per 4-hour block by size (S/M/L/XL), open 7:00–23:59, 96-hour (4-day) storage limit before forced removal. [spacer.co.jp](https://spacer.co.jp/en/base/op-51/) / [coinlocker-shiori.com](https://coinlocker-shiori.com/seibu-shinjuku-station/)
- Underground passage into Shinjuku Subnade mall (listed as Subnade's locker point #1). [subnade.co.jp](https://subnade.co.jp/shop/coinlocker01)

**Inside Kabukicho itself**
- Tokyu Kabukicho Tower (opened 2023) has no *officially advertised* general-purpose locker per its own FAQ (it instead offers a staffed baggage-check desk at the 1F tourist-information counter), but multiple independent visitor-guide sources document three distinct in-building locker installations: a 24h, 300+ unit large locker bank at B1F near the "Kabukicho Ichibangai" arch entrance; small electronic-payment-only lockers on the 4F cinema floor (¥200 small / ¥300 medium, 180-min limit, cinema-goers only); and cashless-only lockers beside the elevators on 10F (109 Cinemas Premium Shinjuku floor). [travel.suiyotei.com](https://travel.suiyotei.com/ja/archives/2463) / [mintoku.ne.jp](https://mintoku.ne.jp/article/785) / [tokyu-kabukicho-tower.jp/faq](https://www.tokyu-kabukicho-tower.jp/faq/)
- Zepp Shinjuku (live-music venue in the Tokyu Kabukicho Tower basement): 840 lockers across B1F (690) and B4F (150); B1F bank (740 units, cash-only ¥400, change machines provided) is the main one. Access is entry-restricted — lockers can only be used *after* passing the venue ticket gate, not as pre-entry luggage storage. [trend-dogman.com](https://trend-dogman.com/zeppshinjuku-coin-locker/) / [aoiro-nikki.com](https://aoiro-nikki.com/zepp_shinjuku_locker/)
- Humax Pavilion Shinjuku Kabukicho (leisure/entertainment building), ~1 min walk from Tokyu Kabukicho Tower: 24h coin lockers, cash payment, small ¥300 / medium ¥400. [aoiro-nikki.com](https://aoiro-nikki.com/zepp_shinjuku_locker/)
- Hotel WAKO Shinjuku, parking-area side, ~3 min from Seibu-Shinjuku Station North Exit toward the "hotel-gai" (love-hotel district): street-level coin locker. [fuji-ya.co.jp](https://fuji-ya.co.jp/coinlocker/coinlockerblog/2001/04/04/西武新宿にコインロッカーを設置-2/)
- Adhoc Shinjuku Building: two locker clusters (2F elevator front, and a stairwell-side bank), ~1 min from Tokyo Metro Fukutoshin Line Shinjuku-sanchome Station exits B8/B9 — on Kabukicho's southern edge. [fuji-ya.co.jp](https://fuji-ya.co.jp/coinlocker/coinlockerblog/2025/06/23/kabukichou-coinlockers-map/)
- Manboo Manboo net-café/net-room (Kabukicho branch): coin locker near the entrance, ~5 min from Seibu-Shinjuku/Higashi-Shinjuku Station. [fuji-ya.co.jp](https://fuji-ya.co.jp/coinlocker/coinlockerblog/2025/06/23/kabukichou-coinlockers-map/)
- Godzilla Road (Kabukicho's main street) locker bank at the entrance of Karaoke-kan Kabukicho Main Store, operated by Fuji Service Co. — a company that operates ~500 coin-locker sites across Tokyo/Kanagawa/Chiba/Saitama. [fuji-ya.co.jp](https://fuji-ya.co.jp/coinlocker/coinlockerblog/2025/06/23/kabukichou-coinlockers-map/) / [fuji-service-cl.co.jp](https://fuji-service-cl.co.jp/)

**Luggage-storage services as a supplement to physical lockers**
- ecbo cloak (smartphone-reservation, coin-locker-priced staffed/shop-based storage) has a confirmed Kabukicho partner store at the H.F. Kabukicho Building, 1F, Kabukicho 1-2-2 — inside the district, distinct from any station locker bank. Suitcases explicitly accepted (a key differentiator from most physical lockers' large-size cap). [ecbo press/site aggregation](https://cloak.ecbo.io/ja/jpn/city/tokyo/8) / [ecbo.io](https://ecbo.io/20250425_keio_departmentstore/)

**Excluded / not confirmed**
- Takashimaya Times Square and Isetan: both are within walking distance of Shinjuku Station but their locker facilities are documented only generically ("shopping complex, south exit") with no distinct, individually citable locker-bank name/address found in this pass — excluded per the no-fabrication instruction rather than guessed.
- A Yakuza-video-game search returned only fictional "Kamurocho" locker/key-item results (not the real district) — explicitly discarded, not used as a source.

**Count reached:** 16 distinct, individually-named/addressed coin-locker or luggage-storage entries — within the requested 15–20 range without padding.

---

### Assumptions

- Exact coordinates were not independently geocoded (consistent with the product's stated "no external API calls" NFR and prior tasks' approach) — all are block/landmark-level estimates derived from named-street/exit proximity, clustered within the same ~300m×400m Kabukicho/Seibu-Shinjuku footprint used in prior deep-dive tasks.
- The Tokyu Kabukicho Tower B1F/4F/10F locker details come from independent visitor-guide blogs, not the tower's own FAQ (which only advertises the 1F staffed baggage desk) — flagged with a lower `reliability_score` and an explicit note that on-site verification is advisable before publishing operating hours/pricing.
- "24h" tags reflect locator/blog-stated hours at time of search, not phone-verified, same caveat as prior tasks (atm, convenience-deep).
- Hotel WAKO and Manboo Manboo entries are sourced from a single blog aggregator (fuji-ya.co.jp) rather than corroborated by a second independent source — flagged with `reliability_score: 2`.

### Hypotheses

- H1: Because Zepp Shinjuku's lockers are entry-gated (usable only after ticket scan), the data model should carry an `access_restriction` field distinguishing "public, walk-up" lockers from "venue-customer-only" ones — otherwise a user could be misled into thinking they can stash luggage there before a show.
- H2: The split between cash-only (Zepp, Humax Pavilion, Godzilla Road/Karaoke-kan, Tokyu B1F) and cashless-only (Seibu-Shinjuku North Exit via SPACER, Tokyu 4F/10F) payment methods is significant enough in this district to warrant a `payment_method` tag (`cash_only` / `cashless_only` / `both`) rather than assuming IC-card ubiquity, since a stranded cash-only tourist and a stranded cashless-only tourist face opposite failure modes.
- H3: ecbo cloak's suitcase-friendly, no-size-cap model makes it the most reliable fallback specifically for the `suitcase_too_big` tag case that physical coin lockers structurally cannot serve (station large lockers max out around a 24" checked bag) — worth surfacing as the primary answer to "my bag doesn't fit any locker" rather than a generic recommendation.

---

---

### Recommendations

1. Ship this as **16 entries**, breaking the old single generic "Shinjuku Station" locker entry into distinct exit-level banks (East/West/South/Lumine) plus the newly found in-Kabukicho locations — this materially improves the product's core value prop (arriving visitors deciding where to stash luggage before exploring).
2. Add an `access_restriction` field (`public` vs `venue_customers_only`) — Zepp Shinjuku's lockers are the clearest case where a naive "coin locker near me" listing would mislead a user who hasn't bought a ticket.
3. Add a `payment_method` tag (`cash_only` / `cashless_only` / `both`) — this district has a genuine split (Seibu-Shinjuku North Exit and Tokyu 4F/10F are cashless-only; Zepp, Humax Pavilion, Godzilla Road, Tokyu B1F are cash-only) and getting stranded on the wrong side of that split is a real failure mode.
4. Do a phone/on-site verification pass on the three Tokyu Kabukicho Tower locker entries and the Hotel WAKO / Manboo Manboo entries before publishing — these are sourced from independent blogs, not the venues' own sites, and carry `reliability_score` 2-3 accordingly.
5. Promote the ecbo cloak entry as the primary answer for the `suitcase_too_big` tag specifically — it's the only entry in this set with no hard size ceiling, filling a gap physical lockers structurally can't cover.
6. Do not add Takashimaya Times Square or Isetan locker entries without a follow-up search targeting their facility pages directly — this pass found only generic mall-level mentions, not individually citable locker-bank details, so they were excluded rather than guessed.

Sources: [shinjukustation.com](https://www.shinjukustation.com/shinjuku-station-lockers-luggage-storage-and-delivery/), [subnade.co.jp](https://subnade.co.jp/shop/coinlocker01), [spacer.co.jp Seibu-Shinjuku North Exit](https://spacer.co.jp/en/base/op-51/), [coinlocker-shiori.com Seibu-Shinjuku](https://coinlocker-shiori.com/seibu-shinjuku-station/), [travel.suiyotei.com Tokyu Kabukicho Tower lockers](https://travel.suiyotei.com/ja/archives/2463), [mintoku.ne.jp Tokyu Kabukicho Tower lockers](https://mintoku.ne.jp/article/785), [tokyu-kabukicho-tower.jp FAQ](https://www.tokyu-kabukicho-tower.jp/faq/), [trend-dogman.com Zepp Shinjuku lockers](https://trend-dogman.com/zeppshinjuku-coin-locker/), [aoiro-nikki.com Zepp Shinjuku / Humax Pavilion lockers](https://aoiro-nikki.com/zepp_shinjuku_locker/), [fuji-ya.co.jp Kabukicho locker map](https://fuji-ya.co.jp/coinlocker/coinlockerblog/2025/06/23/kabukichou-coinlockers-map/), [fuji-ya.co.jp Hotel WAKO](https://fuji-ya.co.jp/coinlocker/coinlockerblog/2001/04/04/西武新宿にコインロッカーを設置-2/), [fuji-service-cl.co.jp](https://fuji-service-cl.co.jp/), [ecbo cloak Tokyo](https://cloak.ecbo.io/ja/jpn/city/tokyo/8), [ecbo.io press](https://ecbo.io/20250425_keio_departmentstore/), [yokoso-shinjuku.com coin locker guide](https://yokoso-shinjuku.com/en/usefull-info/coin-locker/)
